html:
Exclusive to:  

The Bramble Husk is a character-specific Armor Item craftable only by Wormwood. It is found in the Green Thumb Tab, requiring 2 Living Logs and 4 Bone Shards to craft.

It has 450 durability and absorbs 80% of physical damage. Whenever a Character wearing the Bramble Husk is attacked by a Mob, 22.6 average damage (23, 23, 22 respectively three hits) is dealt to each Mob within a radius of one tile. This effect is not triggered by things such as ingesting a Venom Gland or taking damage from Heat, Cold or Hunger.

It also has the added bonus of preventing damage from Spiky Bushes, Brambles, Bramble Blooms, and Bramble Traps. This means that one can harmlessly harvest Spiky Bushes, damage Brambles and Bramble Blooms without any worry, or stand next to a Bramble Trap when it is triggered and not take any splash damage. Durability of the armor is not decreased when this effect occurs. In the Reign of Giants DLC, the player won't take any damage from harvesting Cactus while wearing the Bramble Husk.

In Don't Starve Together, the player won't take any damage from picking Roses while wearing Bramble Husk. It is flammable and can be used as Fuel. Damage multipliers have no effect on damage dealt (Wigfrid's x1.25 damage multiplier, Warly's spicy foods +20% damage bonus, etc.).

Contents

  • 1 ​​ Tips
  • 2 Trivia
  • 3 Assets
  • 4 Gallery

​​ Tips

  • This item is extremely efficient in farming tentacle spikes in the caves by killing tentacle pillars. Its AoE damage kills the tiny tentacles instantly. One husk alone is enough to farm 10 spikes without using helmets, and with minimal lost health, although one should not forget to travel through the tentacle holes, as there is an equal amount of loot on the other side of the hole.
  • This item is renewable as the crafting materials are renewable. Bone shards can be acquired by hammering rotten wobsters. Wobsters can be acquired by fishing using a sea fishing rod with no float and no lure, although adding rot as lure will increase the fishing efficiency by a large amount. This process only results in net gain despite the initial crafting cost of sea fishing rod.
  • This item is very useful in tandem with the Bramble Trap, since using it will let the player avoid their splash damage altogether.
  • Although it shares many similarities with the Cactus Armor included in the Shipwrecked DLC, the Bramble Husk will not prevent an Elephant Cactus from damaging the player.
  • Monsters that tend to swarm together, such as Spiders, Baby Tentacles, Frogs, and Bees can find themselves in a great struggle as the AoE attacks plow through the creatures similar to Abigail.

Trivia

  • This is the only armor that has more durability in Don't Starve Together than in Don't Starve.
  • This is also a great set of armor for quick sanity food as picking cactus will not hurt the player while worn.

Assets

  • Dropped
  • Equipped

Gallery

  • Concept art of Bramble Husk (bottom left) from Rhymes With Play #241.
Tools and WeaponsCraftable Items

Don't Starve
Together
Bramble Husk
"Nature's way of saying 'keep away'."
Object Info
Perk
Prevents damage from Brambles, Bramble Blooms, Bramble Traps and cactus. Picking these things does not wear out the husk. The same affects apply when riding a beefalo with the husk equipped.
Deals 22.6 average damage to every mob around wearer when hit.
450 hp
Does not stack
Protection
80%
Crafting
×2 ×4 ( only)
Tab
Tier
Always available
Miscellaneous
"armor_bramble"
Exclusive to:
“The best offense is a good defense.”
–Wilson
“You mess with me, you get the thorns!”
–Willow
“Mighty Wolfgang does not need armor!”
–Wolfgang
“So my outside matches my inside.”
–Wendy
“DEFEND ME WITH YOUR BODIES, PLANTS”
–WX-78
“The brambles are knit together tightly, though rather clumsily.”
–Wickerbottom
“All natural safety precautions.”
–Woodie
“Who's frail now?”
–Maxwell
“Brambles woven into a makeshift piece of armor.”
–Wagstaff
“Armör studded with fearsöme thörns.”
–Wigfrid
“We're ready to let spikes fly!”
–Webber
“Aww, nature wants to protect me!”
–Walani
“I'll have to be very careful putting it on.”
–Warly
“I'll be a fearsome sight with that thorny hull!”
–Woodlegs
“'TIS ARMOR MADE OF THORNY BITS”
–Wilba
“Pokey protection”
–Wormwood
“I think it looks pretty sharp on me.”
–Wheeler
“Odd design. How do you wear it without pricking yourself?”
–Winona
“Who'd like to give an imp a hug? Hyuyu!”
–Wortox
“Plant made it!”
–Wurt
“No hugging while you're wearing that.”
–Walter
“Sometimes it pays to have a prickly exterior.”
–Wanda
The Bramble Husk is a character-specific Armor Item craftable only by Wormwood. It is found in the Green Thumb Tab, requiring 2 Living Logs and 4 Bone Shards to craft.
It has 450 durability and absorbs 80% of physical damage. Whenever a Character wearing the Bramble Husk is attacked by a Mob, 22.6 average damage (23, 23, 22 respectively three hits) is dealt to each Mob within a radius of one tile. This effect is not triggered by things such as ingesting a Venom Gland or taking damage from Heat, Cold or Hunger.
It also has the added bonus of preventing damage from Spiky Bushes, Brambles, Bramble Blooms, and Bramble Traps. This means that one can harmlessly harvest Spiky Bushes, damage Brambles and Bramble Blooms without any worry, or stand next to a Bramble Trap when it is triggered and not take any splash damage. Durability of the armor is not decreased when this effect occurs. In the Reign of Giants DLC, the player won't take any damage from harvesting Cactus while wearing the Bramble Husk.
In Don't Starve Together, the player won't take any damage from picking Roses while wearing Bramble Husk. It is flammable and can be used as Fuel. Damage multipliers have no effect on damage dealt (Wigfrid's x1.25 damage multiplier, Warly's spicy foods +20% damage bonus, etc.).

​​ Tips[edit | edit source]
This item is extremely efficient in farming tentacle spikes in the caves by killing tentacle pillars. Its AoE damage kills the tiny tentacles instantly. One husk alone is enough to farm 10 spikes without using helmets, and with minimal lost health, although one should not forget to travel through the tentacle holes, as there is an equal amount of loot on the other side of the hole.
This item is renewable as the crafting materials are renewable. Bone shards can be acquired by hammering rotten wobsters. Wobsters can be acquired by fishing using a sea fishing rod with no float and no lure, although adding rot as lure will increase the fishing efficiency by a large amount. This process only results in net gain despite the initial crafting cost of sea fishing rod.
This item is very useful in tandem with the Bramble Trap, since using it will let the player avoid their splash damage altogether.
Although it shares many similarities with the Cactus Armor included in the Shipwrecked DLC, the Bramble Husk will not prevent an Elephant Cactus from damaging the player.
Monsters that tend to swarm together, such as Spiders, Baby Tentacles, Frogs, and Bees can find themselves in a great struggle as the AoE attacks plow through the creatures similar to Abigail.

This is the only armor that has more durability in Don't Starve Together than in Don't Starve.
This is also a great set of armor for quick sanity food as picking cactus will not hurt the player while worn.

Hyuyu! |wurt = Plant made it! |walter = No hugging while you're wearing that. |wanda = Sometimes it pays to have a prickly exterior. |choose = wormwood }} The '''Bramble Husk''' is a [[character]]-specific [[Armor]] [[Item]] craftable only by [[Wormwood]]. It is found in the [[Green Thumb Tab]], requiring 2 [[Living Log]]s and 4 [[Bone Shards]] to craft. It has 450 durability and absorbs 80% of physical damage. Whenever a [[Character]] wearing the Bramble Husk is attacked by a [[Mob]], 22.6 average damage (23, 23, 22 respectively three hits) is dealt to each Mob within a radius of one tile. This effect is not triggered by things such as ingesting a [[Venom Gland]] or taking damage from [[Heat]], [[Cold]] or [[Hunger]]. It also has the added bonus of preventing damage from [[Spiky Bush]]es, [[Bramble]]s, [[Bramble Bloom]]s, and [[Bramble Trap]]s. This means that one can harmlessly harvest Spiky Bushes, damage Brambles and [[Bramble Bloom]]s without any worry, or stand next to a Bramble Trap when it is triggered and not take any splash damage. Durability of the armor is not decreased when this effect occurs. In the ''[[Don't Starve: Reign of Giants|Reign of Giants]]'' DLC, the player won't take any damage from harvesting [[Cactus]] while wearing the Bramble Husk. In ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', the player won't take any damage from picking [[Flower#Rose|Roses]] while wearing Bramble Husk. It is flammable and can be used as [[Fuel]]. Damage multipliers have '''''no''''' effect on damage dealt (Wigfrid's x1.25 damage multiplier, Warly's spicy foods +20% damage bonus, etc.). == ​​{{Pic|32|Prototype}} Tips == * This item is extremely efficient in farming [[Tentacle Spike|tentacle spikes]] in the caves by killing [[Tentacle|tentacle pillars]]. Its AoE damage kills the tiny tentacles instantly. One husk alone is enough to farm 10 spikes without using helmets, and with minimal lost health, although one should not forget to travel through the tentacle holes, as there is an equal amount of loot on the other side of the hole. *This item is renewable as the crafting materials are renewable. [[Bone Shards|Bone shards]] can be acquired by hammering rotten [[Wobster|wobsters]]. [[Wobster|Wobsters]] can be acquired by fishing using a [[Sea Fishing Rod|sea fishing rod]] with no float and no lure, although adding [[rot]] as lure will increase the fishing efficiency by a large amount. This process only results in net gain despite the initial crafting cost of [[Sea Fishing Rod|sea fishing rod]]. *This item is very useful in tandem with the Bramble Trap, since using it will let the player avoid their splash damage altogether. * Although it shares many similarities with the [[Cactus Armor]] included in the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC, the Bramble Husk will not prevent an [[Elephant Cactus]] from damaging the player. * Monsters that tend to swarm together, such as [[Spiders]], [[Tentacle|Baby Tentacles]], [[Frog]]s, and [[Bee]]s can find themselves in a great struggle as the AoE attacks plow through the creatures similar to [[Abigail]]. == {{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * This is the only armor that has more durability in [[Don't Starve Together]] than in [[Don't Starve|Don't Starve.]] *This is also a great set of armor for quick sanity food as picking cactus will not hurt the player while worn. == {{Pic32|Research Notes}} Assets == Bramble Husk Dropped.png|Dropped Bramble Husk Equipped.png|Equipped == {{Pic|32|Blueprint}} Gallery == RWP 241 Green Thumb Tab Concept Art.png|Concept art of Bramble Husk (bottom left) from ''Rhymes With Play'' #241.
